params.text:
What a healthy stoma looks like

A healthy stoma is pink or red, moist and shiny, a little like the inside of the mouth. It may stick out slightly or sit flush with the skin, and its size often settles in the weeks after surgery. A stoma has no nerve endings, so touching or cleaning it does not hurt the person, and it is normal for it to produce a little mucus. A small amount of bleeding when it is cleaned is also normal, as the surface has many tiny blood vessels.

  • Colour: pink or red, moist and shiny.
  • No pain: a stoma has no nerve endings, so cleaning it does not hurt.
  • A little bleeding: a small ooze when cleaned is normal.

Healthy skin around the stoma

The skin around the stoma, called the peristomal skin, should look the same as the skin on the rest of the tummy — intact, and not red, sore, broken, itchy or weepy. Healthy skin is what allows the appliance to stick properly and stay leak-free, so protecting it is central to good stoma care. Each time you support the person, take a moment to look at both the stoma and the skin around it.

Getting the fit right is the key to healthy skin. The opening in the baseplate should match the size and shape of the stoma so that output cannot seep onto the skin, but the baseplate does not press on the stoma itself. Stoma nurses provide a measuring guide or template for this, and the size may need adjusting as the stoma settles. If the skin starts to look sore or the appliance keeps leaking, this is a problem to report, not to ignore.

params.question: Which of these describes a healthy stoma?

answers.text: Pink or red, moist and shiny, with no pain when cleaned and perhaps a small amount of mucus.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Correct. A healthy stoma is pink or red and moist, and it has no nerve endings so cleaning it is painless.

answers.text: Pale, grey or dusky in colour and cold to touch.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: No — a pale, grey or dark stoma can mean a poor blood supply and must be reported urgently.

answers.text: Dry, cracked and flaking on the surface.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: No — a healthy stoma is moist, not dry and cracked.
No — a stoma has no nerve endings, so cleaning it should not hurt. Pain should be reported.
